title: sedum minimum subsp. minimum
intro: Sedum minimum subsp. minimum, commonly known as Goldmoss Stonecrop, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant species that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and anti-aging. Sedum minimum subsp. minimum can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits make it a valuable resource in herbal medicine, and further research is needed to fully explore its potential applications.
